top_signals.title: U.S. hits Iran for ninth consecutive night as American casualties rise
top_signals.summary: The U.S. began a ninth straight night of strikes against Iran, escalating a campaign to destroy Tehran's ability to disrupt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: How much of China’s crude demand will return?
top_signals.summary: With crude benchmarks retreating from above $100/b, market attention is shifting from supply disruptions to the post-crisis rebalancing of global oil markets, despite continued uncertainty over physical trade flows. Setting aside potential logistical disruptions, the global crude balance has already begun to shift. Gulf exports are recovering from recent lows, alternative suppliers continue to load ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Strait of Hormuz Daily Vessel Traffic Down by 60%
top_signals.summary: Following our update last week on changing vessel behaviour in the Strait of Hormuz, we’d like to share AXSMarine’s latest consolidated figures through the full day of 16 July. top_signals.title: Asia-US container rates mostly lower, tanker rates steady to lower
top_signals.summary: Rates for shipping containers from east Asia and China to the US were mostly softer as the frontloading of imports to beat new tariffs is slowing, while liquid tanker rates were flat to lower. top_signals.title: Dry Bulk Shipping: Capesizes Lost Momentum
top_signals.summary: Capesize The market lost momentum over the course of the week, reversing an encouraging start to finish on a notably weaker footing as sentiment deteriorated across both the Pacific and Atlantic basins. top_signals.title: What MEPC 84 means for shipping’s Net Zero ambitions
top_signals.summary: The 84th session of the IMO’s Marine Environment Protection Committee (MEPC84) was always going to tackle the complex decarbonisation of shipping. By its conclusion, MEPC 84 broadly agreed the direction of travel, but the route to get there remains contested. At the centre of the debate is the IMO’s 2023 greenhouse gas reduction strategy, which ...

top_signals.title: VLCC Tanker Rates on the Rise
top_signals.summary: Clean LR2 The TC1 75kt MEG/Japan index moved consistently upward this week to WS432 (+70). A voyage west climbed in value this week with the TC20 90kt MEG/UK-Continent index going from $7.94 million to $8.66 million. In Europe the TC15 80kt Mediterranean/East index held level around its current $4.2 million all week, the corresponding TCE ...
